title: Elon Musk: AI progress is unstoppable, may bring incredible abundance
excerpt: Elon Musk said that AI risks exist but should not halt progress because it could lead to an "age of amazing abundance."
content_html:
Elon Musk says it's worth the risk of killer AI because 'abundance for all' is more likely

By Georgia Hennessy

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Elon Musk says humanity probably shouldn't hit the brakes on artificial intelligence — even if it could.

In an interview with The Economist's editor in chief, Zanny Minton Beddoes, the world's richest man said the risk of AI and robots going catastrophically wrong is "not zero." He said he'd made peace with pressing ahead anyway.

"My sort of philosophical conclusion is to look on the bright side," Musk said. "I can't see any way to really stop this incredible momentum of AI and robots."

"At times I sort of think, well, perhaps even if there was a stop button, we probably shouldn't press it, because the most likely outcome is incredible abundance for all," he said.

Musk predicted AI may exceed the sum of human intelligence "in about five years," leading to "an age of amazing abundance where anyone can have anything they can think of," barring a derailment like "global thermonuclear war."

Musk has long paired warnings of AI catastrophe with predictions that have increasingly blended in more optimism.

He likened building AI to "summoning the demon" in 2014, told an SXSW audience in 2018 that "AI is far more dangerous than nukes," and, as recently as March 2025, put the odds of "killer robots annihilating humanity" at 10% to 20%.

However, his recent predictions have leaned more utopian. In January, he said that saving for retirement would be "irrelevant," and this month predicted that AI and robots will make work optional and fund "universal high income," a claim "The Big Short" investor Michael Burry called "false."

'Minimize the probability of the bad outcome'

In The Economist interview, parts of which aired Thursday morning, Musk was asked if he had decided AI's rise was inevitable.

"Let's enjoy the ride, is my philosophy at this point," Musk replied, saying there was "inexorable progress" in AI and robots and that he couldn't stop it even if he wanted to.

Asked whether he still stood by the 10% to 20% chance of AI going catastrophically wrong, and whether he would board one of his own rockets with that chance of it blowing up, he said: "Yes, but let's say you can't do anything about it. I guess the only thing we can try to do is minimize the probability of the bad outcome."

Musk also said it was unlikely humans would remain in control of AI within a decade. "It would be an exercise in vanity for me to think that I would be controlling supergenius AI that is vastly smarter than me," he said.

"What we can and should try to do is to make sure that the AI has good values, that it cares about humanity, and that it wants us to be happy and prosper," he said.

Musk said he spoke to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis ahead of his frenemy publishing a blog earlier this month on how to regulate AI, and suggested to him that the leading AI execs have regular calls with each other to "discuss any safety and security issues."

"That's where I think the competitors can keep each other honest," Musk said.

Read next

Georgia Hennessy

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Georgia is a fellow at Business Insider's London office.Before joining Business Insider, she worked at Japan's largest newspaper, The Yomiuri Shimbun, and interned at the Financial Times. She is an NCTJ-qualified journalist with a degree in Philosophy from the University of Birmingham. You can contact her via email at ghennessy@insider.com

content_html_zh:
埃隆·马斯克表示，值得冒人工智能杀手的风险，因为“人人富足”更有可能

作者：Georgia Hennessy 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

埃隆·马斯克表示，人类可能不应该对人工智能踩刹车——即使可以。

全球首富在接受《经济学人》主编 Zanny Minton Beddoes 采访时表示，人工智能和机器人出现灾难性错误的风险“并非为零”。 他说无论如何，他已经接受了继续前进的想法。

“我的哲学结论是看到光明的一面，”马斯克说。 “我看不出有什么方法可以真正阻止人工智能和机器人这种令人难以置信的势头。”

“有时我想，好吧，也许即使有一个停止按钮，我们也不应该按下它，因为最有可能的结果是所有人都拥有令人难以置信的富裕，”他说。

马斯克预测人工智能可能会在“大约五年内”超过人类智力的总和，从而导致“一个令人惊叹的丰富时代，任何人都可以拥有他们能想到的任何东西”，除非出现“全球热核战争”这样的脱轨。

长期以来，马斯克一直将人工智能灾难的警告与越来越乐观的预测结合起来。

他在 2014 年将构建人工智能比作“召唤恶魔”，在 2018 年对 SXSW 观众表示“人工智能比核武器危险得多”，并且就在 2025 年 3 月，他认为“杀手机器人消灭人类”的可能性为 10% 到 20%。

然而，他最近的预测变得更加乌托邦。 一月份，他表示为退休储蓄“无关紧要”，本月预测人工智能和机器人将使工作变得可选，并为“普遍高收入”提供资金，“大空头”投资者迈克尔·伯里称这一说法“错误”。

“最大限度地减少不良结果的可能性”

在周四上午播出的《经济学人》采访中，马斯克被问到他是否认为人工智能的崛起是不可避免的。

马斯克回答说：“让我们享受这段旅程，这是我目前的理念。”他表示，人工智能和机器人领域正在取得“不可阻挡的进步”，即使他想阻止，他也无法阻止。

当被问及他是否仍然相信人工智能有 10% 到 20% 的可能性会出现灾难性错误，以及他是否会在有可能爆炸的情况下登上自己的一枚火箭时，他说：“是的，但假设你对此无能为力。 我想我们唯一能尝试做的就是尽量减少不良结果的可能性。”

马斯克还表示，十年内人类不太可能继续控制人工智能。 他说：“如果我认为自己能够控制比我聪明得多的超级人工智能，那将是一种虚荣心。”

他说：“我们能够而且应该努力做的是确保人工智能具有良好的价值观，它关心人类，并希望我们幸福和繁荣。”

马斯克表示，在谷歌 DeepMind 首席执行官德米斯·哈萨比斯 (Demis Hassabis) 本月早些时候发表一篇关于如何监管​​人工智能的博客之前，他与谷歌 DeepMind 首席执行官德米斯·哈萨比斯 (Demis Hassabis) 进行了交谈，并建议他，领先的人工智能高管定期互相通话，“讨论任何安全和保障问题”。

“我认为这就是竞争对手可以保持彼此诚实的地方，”马斯克说。

阅读下一篇

Georgia Hennessy 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Georgia 是《商业内幕》伦敦办事处的研究员。在加入《商业内幕》之前，她曾在日本最大的报纸《读卖新闻》工作，并在《金融时报》实习。 她是一名获得 NCTJ 资格的记者，拥有伯明翰大学哲学学位。 您可以通过电子邮件 ghennessy@insider.com 与她联系

  • AI
